learning_outcomes.description: Understand how and why evolution is an organising principle in the biological sciences.
Know the major evolutionary transitions that have taken place in the history of life, since the evolution of the prokaryotic cell until the present.
Appreciate how the environment is crucial to sustaining life and forcing evolutionary change.
Understand the various processes that contribute to evolutionary change, and how they operate.
Understand the evolutionary history of modern humans, their languages and diseases.
Appreciate the role of the primary literature in developing an understanding of evolutionary biology, and learn how to synthesise ideas from different sources.
Appreciate the nature of the scientific evidence that informs scientific interpretation, and how evidence is gathered and analysed

assessments.category: Notebook/ Logbook, Participation/ Student contribution, Practical/ Demonstration, Quiz, Reflection
assessments.task_description:
The selection experiment will be spread over three practical classes, commencing in week 6 and continuing through until week 8. Assessment will comprise worksheets, one for each practical class and to be completed during class time. Each worksheet will be worth 10 marks and will be marked at the end of each practical class by tutors. The best two of the three worksheets will contribute to a mark worth 15% of the total assessment for the course.

·     Practicals 1 and 2 (set up and analysis), worksheets completed during the practical class

·     Practical 3 — ‘E. coli trivia competition’ (out of 10), answer sheet completed during practical class.

Marks finalised before the in-semester break.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Worksheets for each practical class that form part of the Selection Experiments are to be completed in class and submitted at the end of class.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
The in-semester exam will comprise multiple choice and short answer questions, covering material from Weeks 1–6 (Introduction to Evolution; Natural selection; Co-evolution; Sociality; Sexual selection), along with practical classes 1 and 2 (Phylogenetics and taxonomy). 

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.due_date:
26/08/2026 3:00 pm

Book Club will take place over Week 4 and Week 5 Practicals.
assessments.category: Participation/ Student contribution, Reflection, Translation/ Interpretation, Tutorial/ Problem Set
assessments.task_description:
The Book Club requires students to read a set popular book about evolution. 

In class, students work in pairs and small groups to discuss aspects of the book and build a set of written responses. They will need to pose a question the book raises, weigh the evidence, and defend an argument. 

Book Club will take place over Week 4 and Week 5 Practicals. During week 1 of Book Club, each group will focus on a different chapter of the book, assigned to them at the start of the class, and work through a set of activities.

During week 2 of Book Club, students will complete a worksheet that covers broader concepts developed across multiple chapters, to be submitted at the end of the second practical. The worksheet will be worth 15% of the course.

The task develops your ability to work in small groups and to discuss and communicate complex ideas critically, and clearly.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The worksheet is to be completed in class and submitted at the end of class.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The end of semester exam will comprise multiple choice and short answer questions, covering material from Weeks 7–13 (Speciation; Macroevolution; Origins of life; Diversification; Evolutionary systems biology; Evolution and the fossil record; human evolution), along with questions relating to data obtained during the final two practical classes (Morphometrics and the species problem; Human evolution).

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

The in-semester exam will comprise multiple choice and short answer questions, covering material from Weeks 1–6 (Introduction to Evolution; Natural selection; Co-evolution; Sociality; Sexual selection), along with practical classes 1 and 2 (Phylogenetics and taxonomy). 

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

90 mins + 10 mins planning time

learning_activities.topic:
Exam revision session

Review of material to be covered in the end of semester exam.

The end of semester exam will comprise multiple choice and short answer questions, covering material from Weeks 7–13 (Speciation; Macroevolution; Origins of life; Diversification; Evolutionary systems biology; Evolution and the fossil record; human evolution), along with questions relating to data obtained during the final two practical classes (Morphometrics and the species problem; Human evolution).
